reasoning: The reference value of 1.000 indicates that the preceding 360-day average of 'Battles' in Jordan was essentially 0. For any of the resolution dates to resolve positively, Jordan would need to experience >10 (so 11 or more) 'Battles' within a single 30-day window. While Jordan has faced some recent border clashes and regional spillover from the Israel-Hamas war, the documented frequency of such events is nowhere near 10+ per month. For the earliest resolution date (Nov 16), over 20 days of the observation window have already passed with no reports of an unprecedented military escalation. The risk of a sudden massive spike in armed conflict exists but is deeply improbable over this short timeframe. We establish a baseline probability of ~5% across the board, expanding slightly for further dates reflecting typical forecasting uncertainty horizons.
